name: Puerto Rican Rice with Chicken (Arroz Con Pollo)
recipeIngredient: 2 ounces Lean cured ham (wash and dice salt pork and ham)
6 Fresh cilantro leaves (chop everything into small pieces)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the chicken and divide each piece into two. Dry and rub with seasoning from section B. Set in refrigerator overnight.
2. In a "Caldero" or heavy kettle, heat fat and brown rapidly salt pork and ham. Reduce to moderate and add chicken. Cook for 5 minutes.
3. Reduce heat to low. Add ingredients from section D and sauté for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Meanwhile, drain liquid from the can of peas into a measuring cup and enough water to make 2.5 cups (if regular rice is used) or 3.5 cups (if long rice is used). Reserve peas. Heat the liquid and wait.
5. Add ingredients from section E to the kettle and mix over moderate heat for 2 minutes.
6. Add hot liquid to the kettle and mix well. Cook uncovered over moderate heat until rice is dry.
7. With a fork, turn rice from bottom to top.
8. Cover kettle and cook over low heat for 40 minutes. Halfway during this cooking period, turn rice over again.
9. Add peas, turn rice once more, and cover. Cook for 15 minutes at low heat.
10. Spoon rice into a serving platter.
11. Heat pimientos in their juices, drain, and garnish the rice. Serve immediately.
